mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with rising inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force central banks to tighten, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, driving negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the regime filter adds no incremental IC over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC falls below -0.02 consistently across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand that drags on related equity sectors and produces negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months when HOUST drops below 1200, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: High real interest rates combined with rising unemployment indicate a policy-induced recession risk that rapidly depresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance, or positive returns dominate in >50% of aligned cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annually during a low-inflation environment confirms sustainable expansion that supports positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% in low-inflation periods yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime condition eliminates all predictive power.
